text: Implementing strong security measures is crucial in web development. First, always use HTTPS to encrypt data between the user's browser and your server, protecting sensitive information from interception. Authentication systems, such as requiring strong passwords and two-factor verification, ensure only authorized users can access accounts or data. Input validation is another key practice; checking user-provided information on the server side prevents malicious data injection, like SQL code or scripts, from harming your database or site. Common vulnerabilities, such as cross-site scripting (XSS) and SQL injection, often result from weak validation. By securing HTTPS, managing authentication carefully, and validating all inputs, you significantly reduce these risks.
